lede: A look at December 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Galba and Brigetta Barrett, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Tatmadaw
events.desc: Burmese military forces commit the Mo So massacre, killing at least 44 civilians.

events.title: Lord's Resistance Army
events.desc: The Lord's Resistance Army, a Ugandan rebel group, begins a series of attacks against civilians in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, massacring more than 400.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Pope Pius XII makes a Christmas Eve appeal for peace.

events.title: Argentina
events.desc: Assassination attempt on Argentine President Hipólito Yrigoyen.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The "Christmas truce" begins.

events.title: Italian Hall disaster
events.desc: The Italian Hall disaster in Calumet, Michigan results in the deaths of 73 striking workers families at a Christmas party participants (including 59 children) when someone falsely yells "fire".

events.title: Aida
events.desc: The opera Aida premieres in Cairo, Egypt.

events.title: United Kingdom
events.desc: Representatives of the United Kingdom and the United States sign the Treaty of Ghent, ending the War of 1812.

events.title: Tang dynasty
events.desc: Tang dynasty poet Du Fu departs for Chengdu, where he is hosted by fellow poet Pei Di.

births.name: Nicholas Meyer
births.line: American screenwriter, film director, and author

births.name: Tarja Halonen
births.line: Finnish lawyer and politician, 11th President of Finland

deaths.text: Harold Pinter, English playwright, screenwriter, director, Nobel Prize laureate
Vasco da Gama, Portuguese explorer and politician, Governor of Portuguese India

observances.text: Christmas Eve (Christianity) and its related observances: Aðfangadagskvöld, the day when the 13th and the last Yule Lad arrives to towns. (Iceland)
Christmas Eve (Christianity) and its related observances: Feast of the Seven Fishes (Italian Americans)
Christmas Eve (Christianity) and its related observances: Juleaften (Denmark)/Julaften (Norway)/Julafton (Sweden)
Christmas Eve (Christianity) and its related observances: Nittel Nacht (certain Orthodox Jewish denominations)
